target.name: Percepto
target.founder: Dor Abuhasira, Ariel Avitan, Sagi Blonder, Raviv Raz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $128M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Monarch Tractor
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Percepto is a Series C–stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $128M to date. The comp universe consists of six private peers in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Tekever; no public comparables exist in this narrow category.

The valuation analysis employed stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $128M total raised proxy, yielding $218M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints met narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), with only one adjacent-stage peer identified. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable bias given valuation dispersion across the frontier-tech autonomy landscape.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $185M (low) to $250M (high), with a median of $218M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty given reliance on stage-progression alone and the absence of tightly comparable peer multiples. Key risks to valuation include commercialization pace, regulatory clarity in autonomous deployment, and capital efficiency relative to the broader drone and autonomous systems market. Readers should treat this as a preliminary range pending richer comparable data or updated financial performance metrics.
